prompt: Give me a short pep talk before I start a hard task I have been putting off all morning.
response:
Alright, listen up! You've been staring at this thing all morning, and you know what? That's okay. We all do it. But now? Now is the moment.

Take a deep breath. Seriously, a good, deep one.

You've got this. You're smart, you're capable, and you've overcome tougher things than this before. This task, no matter how big or daunting it feels right now, is just a series of small steps. You don't have to conquer the whole mountain in one go. Just take the first step. Open the document, write the first sentence, make the first call.

The hardest part is always *starting*. Once you're in motion, the momentum will build. You'll find your rhythm, and before you know it, you'll be looking back wondering why you even hesitated.

Think about how good it's going to feel when this is done. That feeling of relief, of accomplishment, of having reclaimed your mental space. That's waiting for you on the other side.

So, let's go. One foot in front of the other. You've got this. Go get it done!
